cap:
After finishing both books I absolutely believe there is a good story in there. Not a masterpiece, but something worth reading. However the amount of shock that’s trying to happen with the rot etc. heavily takes away from what could have been a very emotional story. 

If you have a weak stomach don’t pick this up, it’s not a slight mention and then it goes away. The eww feelings persist through all of book 1 and then only subside partly with book 2. 

Check the TW’s there are plenty here and some dubious consent IMO

The author is trying so hard to make you feel a certain aesthetic that I just didn’t get the plot and character development I wanted. Plus I knew what was happening from the jump. She made it so obvious. This would have been much better as a single book with a slow burn. But I also know that’s not the type of author this book has, so if you want to pick it up at least it’s short and skimmable. 

I also think that all the “reviews” calling this a masterpiece do it a disservice. People get their hopes up.

As always, these are my thoughts and I wouldn’t expect you to have the exact same thoughts all the time. That would be incredibly weird. 🖤

This was a solid Romantasy! I’m not like blown away or super shocked by anything. But I think for someone who wants a little bit of every thing in an easy read, this fits that category. I flew through reading it in a day! The pacing moved along, the MMC was protective and the FMC didn’t feel like a child. 
I think my one critique is how horrible her parents were, felt overdone. I’m kinda over the awful parents trope. 
And if you’ve been reading Romantasy for awhile, you’ll probably pick up what the author is up to, that didn’t necessarily ruin it though. 
Not sure when I’ll pick up book 2, but I might.

“Lie to me, Delaney. Tell me you don’t feel it. I f*cking dare you.”

Delaney has lived a quiet life, hiding from her past at an estate that deals in the reversal of magic. But her sequestering has little to do with the lie of legacy her parents sold. She holds a secret gift. Taboo. Arcane. Previously thought to be as extinct as the owl they pray to. After untimely deaths pull her away from all she knows, Delaney finds herself in a position meant for her sister: bride to a Lord. Head of their faction. But her sliver of hopefulness is shattered by a dream.

Don’t trust him.

Val’s driving desires are inarguably reasonable: for his arranged wife to love him and to use his necromancy—held on a pedestal unlike Delaney’s—to make a difference and set his people free. If only he can get through the veil of denial his darling wife clings to like a lifeline. And the gentle suggestions of his deceased sister-in-law that he is not to be trusted. With his obsession at war with his secrets, Val races against a ticking clock to opportunity, a chance to change the world.

Plus some of the storylines started not continuing, something would get picked up and left behind for new stuff. Still had some interesting magic, dream states, and overall fun vibes. 
I wish that everything just came together better than a sudden group project let’s fix this gang at the end.

cap:
What a fun ride, I am so glad that I have finally completed my Throne of Glass read. And thank you to everyone who came on this journey with me!

Spoiler Free!

Now for a review…
I love the skill SJM has to weave all the little details together. I am in awe of her and all of her series for that skill. I put my rankings of books, men, women and series in my Kingdom of Ash Highlights incase those things are import to you. I’m sure that some of my opinions are definitely not the popular opinion. 

And now that I am finished I can confidently say that if you come to me for advice on this series I’ll tell you to read The Assassin’s Blade first and tandem read Empire of Storms and Tower of Dawn. But you won’t catch me dead arguing if you want to do something different, that’s not my style. 

And while I had an amazing time reading this, it sadly is not that Roman Empire feeling in the end. The chosen one trope is a hard one for me to like and even harder when I didn’t end up loving “the chosen one”. There were some amazing memorable moments all throughout the series. So when I say it’s a 5 star read it’s more that it’s a 5 star series because everything just comes together so nicely. I had a few tears throughout, no major sobbing. 

Oddly enough before going into this I had thought it was a YA Fantasy more. So I was expecting less emphasis on the relationships. But oh no this is definitely a Romantasy! And it’s not YA which is good cause I love a NA book more. ……but I wish more of the “spicy” scenes were even more toned down 😂 while I love spice these felt cringe sometimes and I found myself very uninterested in a lot of the little relationship aspects. The one scene I would have wanted for this actually was off page 🤣 figures! Oh well…

I guess we shall see if this makes my top reads for the year or the month eventually…I don’t know that it would have unseated anyone in my top 12 last year. I have a specific vibe that I love and this isn’t quite it anymore. 

#kingdomofash

PSA: I have seen some people comment that the “eww” factor wasn’t too bad and people should just try reading it. Or it’s just an “eww” to get over… Well the first book has 209 mentions of rot, pustules and maggots. And there are other triggers beyond the “eww” so if that’s something that triggers you, skip these books, do not feel pressured to read books that trigger you. It is that serious and I will delete any comments that don’t take trigger warnings seriously. 

Now was this book better, yes I think it had some more beautiful emotional moments especially in the last 20%. But the amount of time spent “bedding” him to get there was so over the top in my opinion. And while I did cry at the ending, I think that the situation was more inherently sad rather than from a strong connection to the characters. 

I guess it just could have been so much better. And I could have definitely done without that last scene. 

To each their own though, if it worked for you then I’m happy for you.

cap:
Books on my current priority TBR, I’m sure more will pop up that will push some of these. 
What is everyone else planning to read? Anything in common?

The ending was 5 stars, but everything to get there was torture. So settling somewhere in the middle. 

Empire of Storms by Sarah J Maas

Before I get to any spoiler territory, if you have the opportunity to read this and Tower of Dawn as a tandem I highly recommend it. Otherwise I pray for you!

Potential spoilers…

I think that how much you love this book absolutely depends on how much you love the romances developing in this series. And I like some but definitely am not into all of them. And while they sit around and wait for all the puzzle pieces to potentially come together that is what we are left with to fill the time. Personally I love Chaol, but this isn’t his book. So this one has been my least favorite of the series. I get what SJM was up to, but I just felt like I wasted my time for the first 600 pages. And that doesn’t make me super happy. No matter how great I think the ending was.

#empireofstorms

My expectations were too high. After reading Conform in January and giving it 5 ⭐️ I was so excited to jump into this. But everything I thought it would be it was not. And it wasn’t horrible, but I was told yearning and got nothing of the sort. It was just a regular trauma based romance in a dystopian world. Where sure stuff is coming that may be interesting, but that stuff isn’t in this book yet. 
So annoyed. Do I now have to read all this prequel series to enjoy the books that come with the conform trilogy. I guess time will tell. 

You did not have to read Conform to read this.

Beneath comes out March 24th

Was this a good book? Haha nope the writing is barely passing, most of the characters are insufferable including the FMC, they are young and they act like frisky young people all the time, the plot is a hot mess, everything is described in terms of hotness and it’s way way too long. But will I come back for more of this ridiculousness in book 3??? Yes, yes I will. 

I knew what I was getting myself into though, cause book one wasn’t great either. It was however very fun. 

I don’t know that this was quite as fun, but it’s got a trope that is super spoilerey and not my favorite.  
Uhhh I wanted to skim so bad, but that’s impossible in an audiobook. So while the narration was fine and I can’t say too much on it cause ✨spoilers✨ I would probably have chosen to eyeball read this one if possible. 

If you hated the first one, this is round 2 of that. If you absolutely loved the first one then depending on how you feel about ✨spoiler trope✨ you’ll either love or hate this as well. 

Good luck! I cannot wait to see how this goes down for people. 🤣

✨I closed the book, looked up and said “fck”✨

Oh yeah- in case you haven’t seen it the rainbow scale is where I would place some of the popular books I’ve read and how this book compares. It’s my opinion of course. 😉

This was so close to getting 5 stars and then the last ten pages came and made me rethink everything. 
Does that happen to you too? Well it’s been happening to me way too often, and I call it the kitchen sink ending. Where the author decides to throw out random “shocking” info, I assume in the attempt to make me want to read book 2. But I already wanted to read book 2…I didn’t need that BS. 😤

Look tho overall it was great. The book finally had deadly trials and they did not hold back! Sure was the romance super similar to a very popular book cough*fouthwing*cough yes absolutely but I still liked it. And I loved the MMC, when we finally get his POV watch out! 

And I also think this book will get a decent amount of hype. Which is mostly deserved 🖤

….but I swear to fck if the next book is largely based on misunderstanding…which is the way the ending set up. Then I will be so upset. 

Highly recommend the audiobook!

Thank you to @prhaudio for my copy of No Matter What by Cara Bastone 

Firstly I loved the way the narration was done with the two POV’s took me a minute to realize what was going on and the pattern but that came together really beautifully at the end. And the narrator has the perfect voice for the MMC.

The story felt very authentic and had a realistic timeline of coming back together after tragedy. I did also want to shake the characters multiple times over the ridiculous misunderstanding. Ugh why did that have to be such a driving force in this book?! I also loved the art class and all the people she meets there. That part felt a tad exaggerated from someone who’s done a lot of figure drawing classes. But I’ll take it. 

I saw that some people said the ending felt rushed. I don’t know that I felt quite that way but there was a point that I was like oh we are getting near the end due to that scene and then realized there was quite a lot of book left still. So I guess I’m glad it didn’t drag out too long. 

Either way definitely worth reading/listening to!

#nomatterwhat #contemporaryromance

Romantasy lovers, this was a good one! 
Out today A Vow in Vengeance 

I’ve been super fussy about Romantasy books lately. A lot of them have been feeling like they are trying to tick the boxes of certain tropes to get attention instead of making the tropes fit within the world they create. And this one did a better job than most! 

I loved the MMC (he has wings and calls her “love”) and he is down bad for our FMC. The FMC is very head strong maybe a little too much so, but the plot made up for it. So many exciting things are happening in A Vow in Vengeance to keep you interested. 

Definitely worth checking out!

Highly recommend picking this one up!

Really well done story! But loses half a star for taking us so far out of the main plot for so long. I love Chaol though! I wasn’t sure about this book but it really surprised me. Excited to finish the series with Kingdom of Ash. 

Tower of Dawn by Sarah J Maas

So many new exciting characters have been developed in Heir of Fire. And I’m really excited for all the ground work it did to set up what I’ve heard is one of the better books of the series. 

Heir of Fire by @sarahjmaas 

I have so many questions on how the puzzle pieces will fit together in the end as really I’m only about 40% of the way through the series, page count wise. 

Spoilers…

Things I’m excited for…
Our main FMC (which name am I supposed to be calling her by now?) I really can’t wait to see her be a bad ass back home and whip some men from her past into shape. 
Chaol, will he meet up with her or is he off on a tangent? Thank god he took the dog out of there.
Aedion, god I hope she breaks him out spectacularly!
Manon is good, I’m waiting for her to be great and interact with our main characters. 
Dorian, please no more love sick puppy. It’s time to man up, how you will do that while being literally collared I don’t know. But right now I’m not in love with you dude. 
Rowan I don’t know what you should be doing, but I’m sure you’ll do something.
Is it bad that I would love to see a great betrayal? I need something new to hate. 
Trauma, bring me more deaths and all the trauma. 
K thx. Not too much to ask right? 

#heiroffire

Another fun read for the Throne of Glass series with tons of action packed moments. I mean that ending definitely took things up a notch finally. 

Crown of Midnight by @sarahjmaas 

Light spoilers!

In the world of fantasy books it was definitely time to “break up the band” things were getting way too comfortable around the glass castle. I do think there is a tad more romance than I would want for this point in a “fantasy” with a lot more world building to go. And the first half of the book was really overshadowed by it. I don’t dislike either of our current male characters, I just think with an 8 book series. There’s no HEA coming anytime soon. 
One thing I think Sarah J Maas does very well is drop breadcrumbs. Just enough to not leave you shocked when something happens but not too much to leave the reader bored. Definitely something a lot of current Romantasy releases is trying to accomplish and not as seamlessly delivering. 

I’m excited to see where the next book takes us! 

#crownofmidnight

I was genuinely shocked by how completely this plot and these characters pulled me in. We’ve got two women, one prophecy that one of them will lead their people to victory over the other. And then from there everything starts getting connected. Plus there’s a fever that will kill you, but only some are brought back from it with magical powers. 

Thank you to @macmillan.audio for my alc of Seek the Traitor’s Son by Veronica Roth

There are a lot of characters and time changes here, so if you can I highly suggest having a physical or digital copy to go with your audiobook. The narrators do a great job but with 2 FMC and 1 MMC POVs it can be hard to remember when, where and who we are talking about.

Thank you to @prhaudio for my advanced listening copy of Operation Bounce House by Matt Dinnimam

Standalone - you do not have to of read anything in the DCC universe to enjoy this!

This was a fun streamlined non-rpg version of Dungeon Crawler Carl. And yet somehow the deaths in this one hit harder. Not exactly the same as main character deaths in DCC, but that overwhelming feeling of doom and that this is going horribly. Still had a great time and this one was also full of its own unique quirks.

Devoured it in one day! I highly suggest checking out the audio version with Jeff Hays and Travis Baldree. Their range for character performance is unmatched. 

Out February 10, 2026
